title: Delicate Garlicky Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A light and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring lamb, garlic, and red wine jelly, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This recipe is egg-free and friendly for various dietary needs.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the red wine jelly and stir to combine, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Add 1 cup of water to the pot and bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes.
While the lamb is cooking, prepare the khao khuat rice according to package instructions.
To serve, place a portion of the rice on a plate, then top with the lamb and sauce.
